solution.p: We will factor 25t^{2}+10t+1 where a=25, b=10, and c=1.

Perfect Square Formula: The formula for perfect square trinomials is (a \pm b)^2=a^2 \pm 2ab + b^2. Note that the polynomial 25t^{2}+10t+1 matches the formula's right side, and we have: \begin{aligned}[t]25t^{2}+10t+1\amp =\left(5t\right)^2+2\left(5t\right)\left(1\right)+\left(1\right)^2\newline \amp =\left(5t+1\right)\mathopen{}\left(5t+1\right)\newline \amp =\left(5t+1\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (25) \cdot (1) = 25.

The number 25 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(25)\amp -1(-25)\amp 5(5)\amp -5(-5)
ewline\end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of 10, and this sum happens with 5+5=10 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]25t^{2}+10t+1\amp =25t^{2}+5t+5t+1
ewline \amp =5t\mathopen{}\left(5t+1ight)+5t+1
ewline \amp =\left(5t+1ight)\mathopen{}\left(5t+1ight)
ewline \amp =\left(5t+1ight)^{2}\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in 25t^{2}+10t+1 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 25, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(25)\amp 5(5)\amp \end{array}
The third term, 1, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(1)\amp -1(-1)\amp \end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with 25t^{2}+10t+1 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:

Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]25t^{2}+10t+1\amp =\left(5t+1\right)\mathopen{}\left(5t+1\right)\newline \amp =\left(5t+1\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
